title: '10th anniversary of Brexit referendum marks event long thought impossible in the UK: The rise of the far right'
subhead: Widespread discontent over immigration policy, which fueled the 2016 vote to leave the European Union, is now driving a surge in nationalist identity and benefiting Reform UK leader Nigel Farage, notes Le Monde's Philippe Bernard in his column.

title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
'Marc Bloch was persecuted not only by the Nazi occupiers, but also by the French state'
After UK PM Keir Starmer's resignation, Andy Burnham could take over by mid-July
subhead: Less than two years after his victory in the July 2024 elections, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday, June 22. Andy Burnham, elected MP for Makerfield on Friday, has emerged as the clear favorite to succeed him.

title: Germany joins France in KNDS shareholding, paving the way for a stock market listing
subhead: Paris and Berlin have agreed to each hold 40% of the manufacturer of the Caesar howitzer and Leopard 2 tank, in order to 'decide on strategy as equals.' The remaining shares will be sold to investors through a dual listing in Paris and Frankfurt that is expected in the coming days.

title: After long match delayed by thunderstorm, France defeats Iraq to advance to the round of 16
subhead: France beat Iraq 3-0 on Monday in a match that lasted almost four hours due to weather conditions in Philadelphia. Kylian Mbappé, Michael Olise and Ousmane Dembélé all played decisive roles.

title: France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
subhead: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities ensure equal access to places to cool off and to learn how to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.

250 years of American independence: How the text that founded the US – and changed the world – was drafted
subhead: 'America 250' (1/13). The 13 colonies had been fighting the British Crown for a year when, in July 1776, lawyer Thomas Jefferson completed the drafting of the Declaration of Independence. Adopted by Congress on July 4 in Philadelphia, this document, which established the principle of equality for all – even though slavery was still legal everywhere – founded the United States of America.

title: The (not so) stupid questions to ask during a heatwave in France
subhead: Can we swim in the Seine? Should I eat meat when it is 40 degrees? Can I ask for a glass of water in any café? Le Monde answers your questions.

title: Mysterious whale 'necropolis' discovered at the bottom of the Indian Ocean
subhead: A team of researchers has uncovered hundreds of fossilized whale carcasses off the southwest coast of Australia. Spanning 1,200 kilometers, this is the largest, deepest and oldest cetacean bone graveyard ever observed.
